rulings.Case Title: LUIS GONZALEZ VS. ANDREA DEBERRY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Responses To Supplemental Discovery Requests And Set Two Of Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 20, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT 3515-17-19 SACRAMENTO STREET , A CONDOMINIUM, ALICE FARRELLY MOTION To Compel Responses To Supplemental Discovery Requests And Set Two Of Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Plaintiff shall provide verified, code-compliant responses without objections to Defendants' Supplemental Interrogatories - Set One, and Defendants' Special Interrogatories, Set Two within 10 days. Plaintiff shall provide verified, code-compliant responses without objections to Defendants' Supplemental Requests for Production of Documents - Set One, and shall also produce any response documents within 10 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 20, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Evidentiary rulings: Defendant's objections to Plaintiff's evidence: Litov Dec.: sustained. The material is hearsay. Cohen Dec.: sustained on all bases cited. Plaintiff's declaration: 1: improper opinion, no proper foundation for opinion; 2: sustained on all bases cited; 3: overruled; 4-10: sustained on all bases cited. San Juan Deposition: 1-3, 5-8, 10: sustained on all bases cited; 4, 9: overruled; plaintiff's deposition: sustained on all bases cited. Plaintiff's disputation and objections to defendant's evidence, putatively undisputed facts 8, 10, 11 and 13: Overruled. Plaintiff's materials designed to dispute defendant's evidence, to the extent these are admissible, do not in fact dispute defendant's fact. The consequence of the court's evidentiary rulings is that there is no dispute as to the professed undisputed facts and defendant is entitled to judgment as a matter of law. rulings.Case Title: OFFICER JASON KIRCHNER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 20, 2012, Line 10, PETITIONER OFFICER JASON KIRCHNER'S Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus. The Court denies the petition for writ of mandate. The petitioner fails to demonstrate that respondent's findings are against the weight of the evidence, or that respondent's interpretation of SFPD Department General Order 5.02.I.B.1 was unreasonable. The standard of review is independent judgment. "[I]n exercising its independent judgment, a trial court must afford a strong presumption of correctness concerning the administrative findings, and the party challenging the administrative decision bears the burden of convincing the court that the administrative findings are contrary to the weight of the evidence." Fukuda v. City of Angels, (1999) 20 Cal.4th 805, 817. The court does not find that the post-hearing-post-discipline findings are suspicious. Topanga Assn. for a Scenic Community v. County of LA (1974) 11 Cal.3d 506, 516, R's decision must "bridge the analytic gap between the raw evidence and ultimate decision or order" to facilitate judicial review. Indeed, courts often remand matters to administrative agencies so they can clarify their decisions. Both petitioner's and respondent's interpretation of "public place" in GO 5.02.I.B.1 are reasonable. The public can and does access the lunchroom at times and respondent's interpretation furthers the underlying purpose of the GO which is to promote safety. Courts should defer to government agency interpretations of their own regulations. Yamaha Corp. of America v. State Bd. of Equalization (1998) 19 Cal.4th 1, 11-13. The parties dispute whether Sgt. Fabbri directed Petitioner to draw his firearm. Petitioner claims that Sgt. Fabbri told him to do so. (AR 171:8-16). Lt. Santos, who investigated the affair and spoke with Sgt. Fabbri, reported that Sgt. Fabbri never gave Petitioner permission or ordered him to draw his firearm. (AR 122:12-123:10). Officer Lyons stated that "somebody" made the suggestion to w/draw the firearm and Sgt. Fabbri thought that that might be funny. (AR 137:5-140:22). The court defers to the respondent's factual determination as supported by the record. Finally, petitioner argues that the Chief imposed discipline for, among other things, leaving the room with the gun unholstered, whereas the Commission found the weapon holstered by the time petitioner left the room. It is clear from the Commission's findings that it believed the discipline imposed by the Chief was justified by the mere act of displaying the loaded gun, whether or not petitioner thereafter left the room with the gun holstered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: ANGUS BEAN et al VS. THE TRAVELERS INSURANCE COMPANIES INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 20, 2012, Line 11, DEFENDANT THE TRAVELERS COMPANIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted. Defendant has shifted the burden to show that there is no coverage under the applicable policy and Plaintiffs fail to raise a triable issue. Plaintiffs' core argument here is that through its knowledge and implied acquiescence to the actions of Complex in purporting to provide coverage to others, the defendant is estopped from denying coverage now. But the current motion for summary adjudication does not seek to adjudicate that theory of coverage (August 3, 2012 Reply at 1), and it is not decided in this motion. The policy language as such does not extend coverage to the plaintiffs. As far as defendant knew at the time of tender, Maze, not Complex, rented the space at issue. As of the time of the PVA, there is no evidence that Maze was the agent of Complex. Maze was not Complex's employee or volunteer worker, or otherwise covered as a protected person under the insurance contract. Accordingly the motion must be granted with respect to breach of contract (issues 4-7), and the duty to defend (issues 1-3), duty to indemnify (issues 24-26) and bad faith (issues 8-15). The excision of these causes of action eviscerates the bases for punitive damages. (Issues 16-23). The request for a continuance is denied. The court has previously granted a continuance and no good showing for yet another continuance has been made. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCP § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL E. BUTLER VS. LAUREEN PARAGUYA DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Complaint To Add Punitive Damages [Ccp 415.13]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 20, 2012, Line 12, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL BUTLER'S Motion To Amend Complaint To Add Punitive Damages [CCP 415.13]. Denied. The motion is not timely under CCP § 425.13(a), which requires the motion to be brought within 9 months before the date the trial is first set. The trial was first set for 10/15/2012. Further, Plaintiff has failed to establish a substantial probability of prevailing on the merits. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: LISA AINSWORTH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Order For Sale Of Dwelling
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 20, 2012, Line 13, Order To Show Cause Re: Order For Sale Of Dwelling. Respondent makes certain arguments opposing the request for an order to allow the sale of the property. With respect to the contention that the application is untimely (CCP 704.750), only speculation suggests that the application is untimely based upon when respondent received the notice of levy. Second, exhibit B to McKim's reply declaration indicates that the notice was mail-served on 7/16/12 and thus Petitioner timely filed this application one day prior to the 8/10/12 deadline (5 days added per mail service, CCP 1013(a)). Regarding the homestead exemption, it appears that respondents reside in England. Petitioner satisfies her burden under CCP 704.780(a)(1). See declaration from Mary Thomas [filed in San Mateo] showing she resides in England and leased the property to tenants. Cal. Coastal Comm. v. Allen (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 322 (no homestead exemption where debtor leased property because he did not "continuously reside" there within meaning of CCP 704.710(c)). Respondents do not show with competent evidence they are only temporarily living in England. With respect to the argument that not the entire property ought to be sold, the Court notes competent evidence that the funds used to purchase the property came exclusively from respondent husband and are traced to his unauthorized sale of petitioner's stock. Finally, the court considers the argument that no evidence of fair market value (FMV) is shown. When an exemption exists, the Court can only order a sale where the sale price will exceed the exemption plus lien amounts, CCP 704.800(a); 704780(b), which in turn requires evidence of FMV. But where the property is not exempt, this proof is not essential. CCP 704.760 ([contents of application] does not specifically require fair market value information and CCP 704.780(b) appears to indicate that a fair market value determination is needed only where there is an exemption). But as a practical matter, we should have proof of FMV, because under CCP 704.850, the sheriff distributes sale proceeds first to the senior lien holder. Here Union Bank has a deed of trust on the property for $1.1 million, and forcing a sale now for less would be a waste of time. Accordingly, the motion is continued to October 15, 2012. Petitioner is to provide evidence demonstrating the fair market value of the property by October 2, 2012. Respondents may file a supplemental reply by October 9, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Compel Responses To Interrogatories, Demand For Inspection, & To Appear For Deposition; Req. For Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos

Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Plaintiff shall provide verified, code compliant responses without objection to Defendant Rubin Glickman's Form Interrogatories - General, Set One and Defendant Rubin Glickman's Demand for Inspection of Documents - Set One, and produce all documents responsive to the inspection demand within 15 days of entry of this Order. Plaintiff shall further appear for deposition within 15 days of entry of this order. Plaintiff shall pay defendant the sum of $375.00 within 15 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA BUILDING INDUSTRY ASSOCIATION VS. STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Issuance Of Writ Of Mandate; Opening Brief
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 20, 2012, Line 16, PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA BUILDING INDUSTRY ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Issuance Of Writ Of Mandate. Petitioner California Building Industry Association is affected by the actions of the respondent Board in setting new fees including for the Storm Water Program. The Board's actions are challenged here because (Petitioner argues) the new fees are not justified by the reasonably expected costs of the services to be provided by the Board. Petitioner also alleges that the fees are illegally retroactive, and finally that the new fees were enacted without the required majority of the Board voting for them. I reject the first two challenges and endorse the third. {The balance of the tentative ruling is too long for the court's website-based distribution and has been emailed to the parties at addresses previously confirmed by them to be appropriate.} Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: ERNEST CHIN VS. MEDSAM ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Protective Order (Amended); Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 20, 2012, Line 18, PLAINTIFF ERNEST CHIN'S Motion For Protective Order (Amended). Denied with respect to the depositions of Jong and Low. While sympathetic to Plaintiffs' representations that they are elderly and frail, and may experience stress in connection with being deposed, these are not sufficient reasons to block the discovery sought. Assuming that the mediation set for September 21, 2012 is unsuccessful, the depositions of Jong and Low shall take place no later than October 26, 2012, and each shall last for no more than seven hours (and it is hoped the length would be shorter). It is the understanding of the Court that the request to depose Lucille Chin is withdrawn. Granted with respect to the documents, which appear covered by the patient-psychiatrist privilege, which survives the death of the patient. The mental state of the decedent Chin is not directly at stake in this case, the parties already have some evidence of his pertinent state (dementia, etc.), and it is a matter of speculation that the records would contain additional materials useful to the parties. On balance, the motion in this respect should be granted. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY DEWITT VS. CRAZY PROTOCOL COMMUNICATIONS, INC. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 20, 2012, Line 21, DEFENDANTS CRAZY PROTOCOL COMMUNICATIONS, INC. and BRIAN MUIR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. Business and Professions Code section 17259.5 neither requires that Mr. DeWitt plead specific facts regarding the subject emails, nor that Mr. DeWitt allege that Crazy Protocol Communications, Inc. had scienter or intent. Hypertouch, Inc. v. Valueclick, Inc. (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 805, 820 - 822. Specific information on the subject emails may be obtained through discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
